(~alifornia 
~1air Political 
Practices Commission 
October 24, 1988 
Robert A. Philipp 
5950 Imperial Highway, unit 27 
South Gate, CA 
90280 
Re: 
Our File No. I-88-399 
~ear Mr. Philipp: 
You have request€;l advice concerning the legality of 
returning surplus carr~aig~ funds to identifiable contributors 
or using the funds tc reimburse persons who made nonmonetary 
contributions to your campaign. 
Your letter concerns 
application of Elections Code Sections 12400··12404, a subject 
outside the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
Your question is more appropriately addressed to 
the Attorney General's Office. 
Therefore, we have forwarded 
your letter to Assistant Attorney General N. Eugene Hill for a 
response. 
A copy of our letter to Mr. Hill is enclosed. 
DMG:KED:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
~~~ 
.. ~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5L 


September 28, 1988 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Formal Written Advice Request. 
Dear Commissioners: 
Following my election to the South Gate City Council 
(April 12, 1988), my Controlled Committee finds itself with a 
surplus of campaign contributions. 
The Committee would like to 
know if these surplus funds can be given back to various 
identifiable contributors and/or can these funds be used to 
reimburse "inkind" contributors? 
Additionally, during the campaign my Corporation (wholly 
owned by me) made both cash and inkind contributions to the 
Controlled Committee. 
Now the question -- can the Committee 
reimburse my Corporation for its contributions to the tune of 100% 
of the surplus funds remaining in the Committee's bank account? 
In light of the spending limitations of Prop. 73, the 
Committee would like to resolve this issue as quickly as possible. 
RAP:hs 
Sincerely, 
rZ1J(;,( P~7 
ROBERT A. PHILIPP 
5950 Imperial Highway, Unit 27 
South Gate, CA 
90280 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
N. Eugene Hill 
Assistant Attorney General 
1515 K street, suite 511 
P.O. Box 944255 
Sacramento, CA 
94244-2550 
Dear Mr. Hill: 
October 24, 1988 
We are referring to you a letter from Mr. Robert A. Philipp 
concerning disposition of his surplus campaign funds. 
We have 
informed Mr. Philipp that his questions are outside the scope 
of the Political Reform Act and are more appropriately 
addressed to the Attorney General's Office. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
DMG:KED:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 III Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Your letter concerns application of Elections Code Sections 12400-12404, a subject outside the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission.
sections.conclusion: Your question is more appropriately addressed to the Attorney General's Office. Therefore, we have forwarded your letter to Assistant Attorney General N. Eugene Hill for a response.
sections.question_synthetic: Can surplus campaign funds be returned to identifiable contributors or used to reimburse nonmonetary contributors, and can a controlled committee reimburse the candidate's corporation for contributions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction over this matter as it concerns Elections Code Sections 12400-12404, which fall outside the Political Reform Act. The inquiry was referred to the Attorney General's Office for proper handling.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (September 28, 1988) and FPPC declination response with referral letter (October 24, 1988). No detailed Q/C sections; conclusion is primarily a jurisdictional declination. File number I-88-399 corresponds to document ID provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your letter concerns application of Elections Code Sections 12400-12404, a subject outside the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission.

CONCLUSION: Your question is more appropriately addressed to the Attorney General's Office. Therefore, we have forwarded your letter to Assistant Attorney General N. Eugene Hill for a response.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice on Robert Philipp's request regarding the disposition of surplus campaign funds, determining the matter falls under Elections Code provisions outside FPPC jurisdiction and referring the case to the Attorney General's Office.
